advanced_node_fabs.title: Advanced-Node Fabs
advanced_node_fabs.summary: **NAICS 3344** — Semiconductor and Other Electronic Component Manufacturing. Subset: **334413** (Semiconductors and Related Devices Manufacturing) for wafer-foundry and IDM logic production. This brief covers **leading-edge logic foundry**: process nodes ≤ 5nm (N5, N4P, N3, N3P, N2, A16) and the DRAM base layers that feed HBM stacks.
advanced_node_fabs.momentum_summary:
- **TSMC monthly revenue** trended sideways in USD terms Jan-May
- **Wafer ASP** on 3nm reportedly softened ~5-8% from the late-2025
- **Samsung 2nm GAA** yield has reached the low-20s% (per
- **2nm wafer pricing** at $30k+/wafer has held — no signs of
- **Apple A20 / A20 Pro** N2 tape-out is on schedule, and Qualcomm
advanced_node_fabs.resolution_summary:
- **TSMC N2 (2nm) ramp** — trial production H1 2025, mass
- **TSMC N3P / N3X capacity expansion** — Fab 21 (Arizona)
- **Samsung 2nm GAA** — mass production H2 2025 (claimed) with
- **Intel 18A** — Fab 52/62 (Arizona) ramp 2026-2027; Microsoft and
- **JASM (Japan Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing, TSMC JV)**
advanced_node_fabs.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKED** (transitioning from PEAKING over the last 6
months, not yet RESOLVING because the segment is still above
mid-70s in B-level terms).

Confidence: **Medium-High**. The combination of 3nm loosening,
sustained 2nm tightness, and a clear 12-24 month relief pipeline
(N2 ramp, Samsung 2nm, Intel 18A first revenue) makes PEAKED the
highest-conviction call. Lower confidence than the HBM call (see
`hbm-memory.md`) because the 2nm-HBM4-CoWoS-L triple overlap in
2026 is an unresolved timing risk that could push the segment
back to PEAKING if any of the three ramps slips materially.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) Intel 18A 2026 ramp
delay, (b) Samsung 2nm yield stagnation below 30%, (c) any TSMC
Arizona utility (water / power) disruption. None currently flagged
as imminent.

advanced_packaging.title: Advanced Packaging (CoWoS / 2.5D / 3D)
advanced_packaging.summary: **NAICS 3344** (Semiconductor and Related Devices Manufacturing), sub-segment **334413A** OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) plus the in-house advanced packaging line at foundries (TSMC CoWoS / SoIC). Covers **2.5D interposer-based packaging** (CoWoS-S, CoWoS-R, CoWoS-L, Intel EMIB), **3D stacked die** (TSMC SoIC, AMD 3D V-Cache, Samsung X-Cube), and **chiplet integration substrates** (ABF-based). Excludes traditional wire-bond QFN/BGA packaging.
advanced_packaging.momentum_summary:
- **TSMC management commentary** on Q1 2026 earnings reaffirmed
- **ABF substrate order book** at Ibiden, Shinko, Unimicron
- **Amkor Arizona** is now in volume production for Apple
- **NVIDIA Rubin tape-out and ramp timing** — Rubin is a
- **Hyperscaler ASIC volume** — Google TPU v7p/v7e, AWS
advanced_packaging.resolution_summary:
- **TSMC CoWoS-L capacity ramp** — Kaohsiung AP6 + AP7 fabs
- **TSMC AP3 (Hsinchu) and AP5 (Taichung)** — both at
- **Amkor Arizona** — Phase 2 advanced packaging ramp Q3
- **Amkor Korea (Cheonan) and Amkor Vietnam** — additional
- **ASE Kaohsiung (K12B)** — first advanced packaging line
advanced_packaging.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKING** (segment tightness still in the upper
range, but the rate of tightening has stalled; the binding
constraint has shifted from "any advanced packaging" to
specifically "CoWoS-L with large interposer, for Rubin-class
products").

Confidence: **High** on the call that this is PEAKING rather
than PEAKED. The CoWoS-L / Rubin-specific intensification
combined with the AI capex backdrop means the segment
tightness is not yet declining in absolute terms. The plateau
character of the last 6 months argues against a PEAKING → PEAKED
transition in the next quarter, but it also argues against
further significant tightening.

This is the single most important segment in the
**long-basket hard guard** framework: it is NOT in RESOLVING,
so it remains fully eligible for the long basket. B × |B'|
ranks it high because B is near peak and B' is near zero
(plateau = high B with non-trivial |B'| signal).

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) AP7 commissioning
delay past Q4 2026, (b) Rubin launch slip into 2027, (c) a
surprise drop in hyperscaler ASIC demand. None currently
flagged.

cooling_water.title: Cooling & Water
cooling_water.summary: **NAICS 333415** (Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment and Commercial and Industrial Refrigeration Equipment Manufacturing) for chillers and CRAC/CRAH. **NAICS 333911** (Pumps and Pumping Equipment Manufacturing) for pumps. **NAICS 3251** (Basic Chemical Manufacturing) for water treatment chemicals.

- **Trane / Carrier / Johnson Controls commercial
- **Ecolab industrial / water segment growth** is
- **Chiller lead times** have stabilized at 50-80
- **Liquid-to-liquid (L2L) CDUs** at hyperscale are
- **Water treatment chemicals** at hyperscale sites
cooling_water.resolution_summary:
- **Trane / Carrier / Johnson Controls chiller
- **Vertiv / Boyd / CoolIT / Asetek CDU capacity**
- **Ecolab / Solenis / Veolia water-treatment
- **Immersion fluid supply** — Shell S5/Immersion
- **PUE / WUE efficiency mandates** drive investment
cooling_water.regime_call_md:
**Regime: STABLE** at the chiller / CDU / water
treatment layer; **EMERGING** at the immersion
cooling layer. Net segment regime: **STABLE** with
EMERGING sub-trend.

Confidence: **Medium-High**. The chiller / CDU
layer is a clear STABLE call (lead times shortening,
growth rate decelerating, capacity being added).
The immersion sub-layer is EMERGING but too small
to flip the segment-level call.

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains
fully eligible for the long basket.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) a major
US drought / water-restriction order that
re-constrains water-availability siting (could push
to PEAKING), (b) hyperscaler immersion adoption
acceleration (would push to PEAKING), (c) Trane /
Carrier / Johnson Controls capacity constraint
emergence.

data_center_shell.title: Data Center Shell (Colo + Hyperscaler Self-Build)
data_center_shell.summary: **NAICS 531120** (Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings) for shell / wholesale data center REITs; **NAICS 5182** (Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services) for the hyperscaler self-build asset class. Covers **colocation REITs** (EQIX, DLR, AMT, IRM, CCI), **wholesale / build-to-suit** operators (Stack, QTS, CyrusOne via KKR, Vantage, Aligned, EdgeConneX), and **hyperscaler self-build** capacity (MSFT, GOOG, AMZN, META, ORCL, Apple, Meta, CoreWeave). Excludes power generation (covered in `power-generation-oem.md`) and cooling (covered in `cooling-water.md`).
data_center_shell.momentum_summary:
- **EQIX Q3 2025 backlog** $5.7B with 33% from AI
- **DLR Q3 2025 backlog** $7.2B with $1.4B in new
- **Hyperscaler capex** 2026 ~$400B is up ~30% YoY
- **Power availability** remains the binding
- **Transformer delivery** at 128+ week LPT lead
data_center_shell.resolution_summary:
- **Hyperscaler self-build** (MSFT, GOOG, AMZN, META,
- **PPA-driven behind-the-meter generation** (Talen
- **New colo development** (Stack, QTS / Blackstone,
- **Three Mile Island restart** (Microsoft
- **SMR / AP1000 commissioning** (TerraPower Natrium
data_center_shell.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKING** (segment tightness still in the
upper range; the rate of new-lease signing is
decelerating but absolute MW pipeline still growing).

Confidence: **Medium-High**. The shell is structurally
a PEAKING segment because it inherits PEAKING from
both upstream tracks (CoWoS + HBM on the silicon
side, transformers + power on the infrastructure
side). The only way the shell resolves faster than
its upstreams is if hyperscaler capex moderates
sharply, which is not yet signaled.

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains
fully eligible for the long basket. For the short
basket, B × |B'| is high.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) a
major hyperscaler capex guidance cut for 2027,
(b) successful PJM / ERCOT interconnection queue
reform (FERC Order 2023), (c) Three Mile Island
restart acceleration, (d) any large-scale
behind-the-meter PPA financial close.

gpu_asic_silicon.title: GPU / ASIC Silicon
gpu_asic_silicon.summary: **NAICS 3344** (Semiconductor and Related Devices Manufacturing), sub-segment **334413D** (Fabless designers of GPUs, AI ASICs, and data-center accelerators). Covers **discrete GPU/ASIC silicon** for AI training and inference: NVIDIA (H100, H200, B100, B200, GB200, GB300, Rubin), AMD (MI300X, MI325X, MI400, MI450), Intel (Gaudi), and hyperscaler in-house ASICs (Google TPU, AWS Trainium, Microsoft Maia, Meta MTIA). Excludes CPU silicon (covered in `advanced-node-fabs.md`) and excludes FPGAs and embedded AI accelerators (covered in `networking-interconnect.md` where the design overlap is heavy).
gpu_asic_silicon.momentum_summary:
- **NVIDIA data center revenue** Q1 FY2027 (reported
- **Hyperscaler capex** 2026 guidance (~$400B) is
- **Hyperscaler ASIC attach rate** is rising: combined
- **Google TPU v7p / v7e** ramp at TSMC N3, plus
- **Sovereign AI** is now a real demand vector: Saudi
gpu_asic_silicon.resolution_summary:
- **NVIDIA Rubin ramp** H2 2026 — the next-generation
- **Hyperscaler ASIC diversification** — the structural
- **Sovereign AI capex** — adds demand, not relief.
- **Neocloud funding slowdown** — CoreWeave's Q4 2025
- **Hyperscaler capex moderation** — none yet in 2026.
gpu_asic_silicon.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKING** (NVIDIA-frontier plateau) transitioning
toward STABLE as the ASIC layer diversifies supply. The
segment is **NOT** in a single uniform regime — the
scoring engine should treat the three sub-layers
(NVIDIA-frontier, hyperscaler-ASIC, sovereign-AI/neocloud)
separately and compute a weighted aggregate.

Confidence: **Medium**. The segment is past peak growth
rate (B' is now slightly negative) but the absolute
spend level is still at record highs. The transition
from PEAKING to PEAKED over the next 6-12 months is the
base case, conditional on Rubin launching on schedule
and no hyperscaler capex cut.

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains
fully eligible for the long basket. For the short basket,
B × |B'| is high but the regime is still PEAKING (not
RESOLVING-from-low or RESOLVING), so short-basket
inclusion is the dominant ranking signal here, not the
regime label itself.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) hyperscaler
capex guidance cut for 2027, (b) NVIDIA Rubin launch
delay, (c) China export control enforcement
intensification, (d) any major sovereign AI project
cancellation.

hbm_memory.title: HBM Memory
hbm_memory.summary: **NAICS 3344** (Semiconductor and Related Devices Manufacturing), sub-segment **334413B** (Memory chips, including DRAM and stacked variants). HBM is a specialized DRAM stack — 8-Hi, 12-Hi, 16-Hi — vertically integrated using TSVs. Covers **HBM3, HBM3E, HBM4, and HBM4E** in production or pre-production.
hbm_memory.momentum_summary:
- **HBM ASP** has continued to rise through 2025-2026; sell-side
- **Per-GPU HBM content** is the dominant demand driver: B200
- **Hyperscaler ASIC demand** — Google TPU v7p/v7e, AWS
- **Samsung HBM4 qualification** with NVIDIA has slipped
- **SK hynix M15X** Cheongju fab is on schedule for first
hbm_memory.resolution_summary:
- **SK hynix M15X** (Cheongju, Korea) — first clean room
- **SK hynix M16** (Yongin, Korea) — first phase under
- **Samsung HBM4 qualification** with NVIDIA — still TBD;
- **Micron HBM4 ramp** — H2 2026 targeted; adds ~10-15% to
- **CXMT (China)** — HBM3-equivalent in development; 2-3
hbm_memory.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKING** (segment tightness still in the upper
range; rate of tightening has decelerated but not yet
reversed).

Confidence: **High**. This is the cleanest PEAKING call in
the value chain. The combination of (a) record ASPs, (b)
per-GPU HBM content still rising, (c) Samsung HBM4
qualification still pending, (d) hyperscaler ASIC demand
additive — all argue for PEAKING rather than PEAKED or
RESOLVING. Confidence is highest on this segment because
the supply-side relief pipeline is well-known (M15X, M16,
Micron, Samsung) and none of the named events have
commenced.

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains fully
eligible for the long basket. For the short basket, B × |B'|
ranks it among the highest in the value chain.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) Samsung HBM4
qualification with NVIDIA, (b) M15X ramp delay, (c) Rubin
launch delay. Any of these would shift the regime toward
PEAKED → RESOLVING over a 6-12 month window.

networking_interconnect.title: Networking & Interconnect
networking_interconnect.summary: **NAICS 3342** (Communications Equipment Manufacturing) for switches and optical, plus **NAICS 3344** for the underlying switch ASICs and PHYs. Covers **scale-up fabric** (NVLink, NVSwitch, UALink, Infinity Fabric) and **scale-out fabric** (Spectrum-X Ethernet, Arista Etherlink, Cisco Nexus, Broadcom Tomahawk / Jericho, Marvell Alaska / Inphi). Includes **optical transceivers** (800G, 1.6T DR8/FR4, LPO, CPO) and **high-speed PCB / substrate** as a binding sub-input.
networking_interconnect.momentum_summary:
- **800G optical transceivers and switch ASICs (N5/N3):**
- **1.6T optical and Co-Packaged Optics (CPO):** still
- **UEC 1.0** spec released June 11, 2025; multi-vendor
- **Optical transceiver ASP** has stabilized at 800G
- **1.6T qualification yields** at all four major
networking_interconnect.resolution_summary:
- **1.6T transceiver yield ramp** at Innolight, Coherent,
- **Coherent / Lumentum / Innolight capacity expansion** —
- **TSMC N3 capacity for switch ASICs** — Tomahawk 5,
- **CPO production at scale** — NVIDIA Spectrum-X
- **NVIDIA ConnectX-8 and BlueField-4** ramp H2 2026
networking_interconnect.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKING** for 1.6T / CPO specifically;
**STABLE** for 800G / N5-class switch ASICs; net
**PEAKING** for the segment with a 1.6T-specific
sub-regime label.

Confidence: **Medium**. The segment-level call is
directionally clear (mildly tightening at the 1.6T
layer, loosening at the 800G layer), but the timing
of the CPO transition creates a 2026 inflection
uncertainty. The scoring engine should track the
1.6T and 800G sub-layers separately and not collapse
them into a single segment-level B / B' read.

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains
fully eligible for the long basket.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) 1.6T
transceiver yield improvement acceleration, (b) CPO
production slip, (c) hyperscaler networking mix
shift (more Ethernet, less InfiniBand), (d) any
Broadcom / Marvell / Cisco market-share surprise
at the switch-ASIC layer.

power_generation_oem.title: Power Generation OEM
power_generation_oem.summary: **NAICS 333611** (Turbine and Turbine Generator Set Units Manufacturing) for gas and wind turbines; **NAICS 2211** (Electric Power Generation, Transmission, and Distribution) for utility-owned generation; **NAICS 3345** (Navigational, Measuring, Electromedical, and Control Instruments Manufacturing) for grid controls. Covers **gas turbines** (GEV, Siemens Energy, Mitsubishi HI), **nuclear steam supply and SMRs** (Westinghouse, BWXT, Holtec, NuScale, X-energy, TerraPower), **wind OEMs** (Vestas, Siemens Gamesa, GEV, Goldwind), **solar + inverters** (First Solar, Jinko, Trina, Enphase, Array Tech), and the **rare earths upstream** (MP, Lynas, USA Rare Earth). Excludes T&D utilities (covered in `transformers-tnd.md` / `td_utilities.md` analogues) and transformers themselves (covered in `transformers-tnd.md`).
power_generation_oem.momentum_summary:
- **GEV backlog** at record levels through 2026-2027;
- **Siemens Energy backlog** similar pattern.
- **Mitsubishi HI** M501JAC + M501G orders up
- **Gas turbine ASP** reportedly >$2,000/kW for
- **Three Mile Island restart** (Microsoft
power_generation_oem.resolution_summary:
- **GEV HA-class gas turbine ramp** — record backlog

- **Mitsubishi HI M501JAC / M501G** — same pattern.

- **Vogtle 3 / 4 (already commissioned)** — adding
power_generation_oem.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKING** (gas turbine layer still
tightening); **EMERGING** (SMR / nuclear restart /
behind-the-meter PPAs adding capacity but not yet
at meaningful scale); **STABLE** (wind / solar
supply / demand balance).

Confidence: **High** on the gas turbine PEAKING
call; **Medium** on the SMR / nuclear restart EMERGING
call (timing uncertainty in 2028-2030 window is large).

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains
fully eligible for the long basket. For the short
basket, B × |B'| ranks the gas turbine layer among
the highest in the value chain.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) Three
Mile Island restart acceleration, (b) gas turbine
ASP softening (would signal peak), (c) any major
SMR licensing milestone, (d) hyperscaler capex cut
in 2027 guidance, (e) Section 232 / IRA
modification that changes gas vs. nuclear /
renewables economics.

rack_scale_integration.title: Rack-Scale Integration
rack_scale_integration.summary: **NAICS 3341** (Computer and Peripheral Equipment Manufacturing) for integrated rack systems; **NAICS 4236** (Electrical and Electronic Goods Merchant Wholesalers) for the integration / distribution layer. Covers **rack-level integration** of GPU/ASIC systems, including NVIDIA HGX / DGX / NVL72, Supermicro rack-scale products, Dell PowerEdge XE racks, and the thermal / power / fabric integration that turns a box of GPUs into a deployable AI-cluster rack. Excludes pure server OEM/ODM (covered in `systems-rack-scale.md`); the distinction is the integration layer: rack-scale includes liquid cooling plumbing, busbars, NVLink switch trays, fabric management, and the rack-level power shelf (the "8 of the 12 kW per GPU" ecosystem).
rack_scale_integration.momentum_summary:
- **Vertiv Q3 2025 backlog** $9.7B was a record, but
- **CDU capacity expansion** at Vertiv (Westerville OH
- **Liquid cooling pricing** has stabilized; channel
- **800V HVDC** is the load-bearing 2026-2027 capex
- **Vertiv 800V HVDC product line** is in
rack_scale_integration.resolution_summary:
- **CDU capacity expansion** at Vertiv, Boyd, CoolIT,
- **800V HVDC power shelf** at Eaton, ABB, Vertiv,
- **Liquid-to-liquid (L2L) CDU adoption** at hyperscale
- **Busbar capacity** at Eaton, ABB, Schneider, Vertiv
- **NVL72 pod growth** continues to drive per-rack
rack_scale_integration.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKED** (CDU layer plateauing and starting
to loosen, with the 800V HVDC ramp as a sub-regime
that keeps the segment from going to RESOLVING for
another 12-24 months).

Confidence: **Medium**. The Vertiv backlog commentary
shift is a strong signal at the CDU layer, but the
800V HVDC ramp creates a 2026 inflection uncertainty.
The scoring engine should treat the CDU and 800V
HVDC sub-layers separately.

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains
fully eligible for the long basket.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) CDU
capacity ramp acceleration, (b) 800V HVDC product
launch slip, (c) any major liquid-cooling pricing
concession from Vertiv, Schneider, or Boyd, (d)
hyperscaler NVL72 ordering pause.

systems_rack_scale.title: Systems OEM/ODM
systems_rack_scale.summary: **NAICS 3341** (Computer and Peripheral Equipment Manufacturing) for server-level systems; **NAICS 3342** for network and communications equipment, but for integrated rack-scale systems the primary code is 3341. Covers **AI server OEMs** (Supermicro, Dell, HPE, Lenovo) and **AI server ODMs** (Foxconn / Hon Hai, Quanta, Wiwynn, Inventec, Wistron, Pegatron). The ODM / OEM split is a long-standing distinction: ODMs design + manufacture, OEMs brand + sell.
systems_rack_scale.momentum_summary:
- **Foxconn monthly revenue** Q1 2026 grew ~25% YoY in
- **Quanta monthly revenue** similar pattern.
- **Wiwynn monthly revenue** similar pattern; first
- **SMCI book-to-bill** has moved from >1.5x in 2024
- **Supermicro rack-scale products** are gaining
systems_rack_scale.resolution_summary:
- **Foxconn + Quanta + Wiwynn + SMCI capex** for
- **ODM consolidation** if it happens would
systems_rack_scale.regime_call_md:
**Regime: STABLE** (segment is at a plateau with no
clear directional signal; the EMERGING sub-trend at
the rack-scale boundary is a watch-item, not a
regime-level call).

Confidence: **Medium-High**. The ODM segment is
structurally a derivative of the GPU/ASIC segment
and does not have an independent tightness /
loosening dynamic. The scoring engine should treat
the ODM segment as a STABLE pass-through with mild
EMERGING signal at the rack-scale boundary.

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains
fully eligible for the long basket — but it is also
not a PEAKING call, so B × |B'| ranks it lower than
the upstream GPU/ASIC, CoWoS, HBM segments for
short-basket purposes.

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a) NVIDIA
shipment acceleration (would push ODM to PEAKING
again), (b) ODM consolidation announcement, (c)
hyperscaler direct-from-ODM bypassing (which would
shift value share but not regime).

transformers_tnd.title: Transformers & Switchgear (T&D)
transformers_tnd.summary: **NAICS 335311** (Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing) and **NAICS 335313** (Switchgear and Switchboard Apparatus Manufacturing). Covers **large power transformers (LPT)** for transmission (>100 MVA), **distribution transformers** (DT) for last-mile, **pad-mount and pole-mount transformers** for utilities, and **gas-insulated switchgear (GIS)** for substation switching. Excludes power generation equipment (covered in `power-generation-oem.md`) and the utility itself (T&D utilities are in the value chain but not in this brief).
transformers_tnd.momentum_summary:
- **LPT lead time** at 128+ weeks (some quotes
- **HUBB / ETN / GEV / Schneider / Siemens Energy
- **Hitachi Energy** is the dominant LPT supplier
- **Cleveland-Cliffs GOES** pricing has held; the
- **DOE Transformer Procurement / Strategic
transformers_tnd.resolution_summary:
- **Hitachi Energy** LPT capacity expansion (US
- **GEV** power transformer capacity expansion at

transformers_tnd.regime_call_md:
**Regime: PEAKED** (segment tightness at peak; the
rate of further deterioration has slowed; the
relief pipeline is becoming visible).

Confidence: **Medium-High**. This is a clean PEAKED
call. The 128+ week LPT lead time has held but
not extended further; the order book is at record
but the order intake rate is decelerating; the
relief pipeline (Cleveland-Cliffs GOES line,
Hitachi Energy, GEV, Eaton, Hubbell) is becoming
visible. The remaining uncertainty is in the
*timing* of the GOES line Q4 2026 commissioning
and the Hitachi Energy capacity ramp; both are
named 2026-2027 events with high conviction.

This segment is **NOT** in RESOLVING, so it remains
fully eligible for the long basket. For the short
basket, B × |B'| is high (high B with mildly
positive B').

Watch-items that would flip the regime: (a)
Cleveland-Cliffs GOES line Q4 2026 commissioning
delay, (b) Hitachi Energy capacity expansion
slip, (c) any major transformer OEM bankruptcy or
M&A event, (d) a 2027 hyperscaler capex cut, (e)
Section 232 tariff outcome (if it raises GOES
import cost, the relief pipeline extends).
